title: About this build · Mockup for review
seo_description: Tech-stack demonstration of the Nebius Builders Network. Not affiliated with Nebius.

blocks.title: About this build
blocks.description: This site is a tech-stack mockup of opencolin/nebius-builders rebuilt on Nebius.com's exact stack — Next.js Pages Router, Gravity UI + Page Constructor, Directus CMS, Typesense, Azure Front Door deploy. The goal is to make the Nebius web team comfortable adopting and maintaining the Builders portal without context-switching from how nebius.com works today.

items.title: Brand chassis
items.text: Mirrors nebius.com: warm off-white surface, navy text + buttons, Inter type, lime joy color on selection. Same g- and pc- class signatures.

items.title: CMS-driven
items.text: Five marketing pages (home, builders, office-hours, localhosts, signup) live as Page Constructor JSON in Directus. Edit them in the Directus admin and ISR republishes.

items.title: Bespoke pages
items.text: Eight data-driven pages (events, leaderboard, library, projects, team and detail routes) read directly from Directus collections via the typed SDK.

blocks.title: Architecture
blocks.description: A request to /any-page traces this path: Browser → Azure Front Door (edge cache) → Next.js origin (Container Apps) → Directus over HTTPS for CMS pages or collection items → renders Page Constructor blocks or bespoke Gravity UI components → returns HTML cached with s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300 (matches the cache header on nebius.com today). Search hits a separate /api/search proxy that holds the Typesense search-only key server-side. Marketing analytics are gated behind a consent banner and only fire after acceptance.

children.title: 1. Edge
children.text: Azure Front Door — global CDN with route-shape cache rules. /_next/static/* one-year immutable, HTML 60s + 5min SWR, /api/* no-store. Mirrors nebius.com's x-azure-ref topology.

list.text: az afd endpoint purge for cache busts

children.title: 2. Application
children.text: Next.js 14.2 Pages Router on Container Apps. Webpack (not Turbopack) + React 18. Standalone output for the prod image; default output for `next start` locally.

list.text: Bespoke Pages Router files for data-driven views

children.title: 3. Content
children.text: Directus 11.17 self-hosted with Postgres 16 + PostGIS for the events.location field, Redis for the cache. Schema is hand-authored YAML and applied via the SDK.

list.text: Idempotent seed script via npm run seed

children.title: 4. Search
children.text: Typesense Cloud in prod, Docker container locally. The admin key never leaves the indexer; the browser hits /api/search which talks to Typesense with a search-only key.

children.title: 5. Auth
children.text: Directus's built-in JWT auth, three roles. Tokens stored in httpOnly cookies; access token rotates transparently via the refresh token in getServerSession.

children.title: apps/web/
children.text: The Next.js Pages Router app. pages/ for routes, src/components/chrome for the layout primitives (PublicNav, Footer, MockupBanner), src/lib for typed Directus and Typesense clients, src/styles/globals.scss for the Nebius brand layer over Gravity tokens.

children.title: apps/directus/
children.text: Directus tooling. snapshots/schema.yaml is the versioned schema. scripts/{apply-schema,snapshot,seed}.mjs handle apply, regenerate, and idempotent seeding. seed/ holds JSON fixtures + ported markdown for library articles.

children.title: infra/
children.text: docker-compose.yml for local dev (Postgres, Redis, Directus, Typesense, mailcatcher). Dockerfile.web is the multi-stage standalone build. azure/ has Bicep skeleton and a README walking through the az deployment commands. typesense/sync.mjs is the indexer.

children.title: Live data layer
children.text: Directus is fully wired. Schema applied, content seeded, every page reads through the typed SDK.

children.title: Auth
children.text: Login flow ends at Directus's /auth/login with HTTP-only cookies and transparent refresh.

children.title: Search
children.text: Typesense client + indexer + /api/search proxy.

list.text: Search-only API key never leaves the server
Header search box hits the proxy

children.title: Local dev
children.text: Docker Compose brings up Postgres, Redis, Directus, Typesense, mailcatcher.

children.title: Portal & admin pages
children.text: One worked example for each (/portal, ready for the requireRole('admin') admin pages). The remaining 14 portal + 8 admin pages from the upstream repo aren't ported yet — the patterns are set, the work is mechanical.

children.title: Marketing analytics
children.text: GTM bootstrap with consent gating is wired in _app.tsx, but no GTM container ID is set. Drop NEXT_PUBLIC_GTM_ID in env to fire it. HubSpot/Demandbase/Hotjar/Clarity placeholders sit in CSP.

children.title: Hero decoration
children.text: Nebius.com's hero has a custom lime-and-green wave SVG overlay. We use a clean Page Constructor header-block instead — adding the wave as a custom block is a future polish task.

children.title: Azure deploy
children.text: Bicep skeleton at infra/azure/main.bicep with inline notes for the resources to define (Container Apps, Postgres Flexible, Redis, Front Door cache rules). Needs a real subscription + region + image registry to deploy.

items.title: GitHub issues
items.text: Open an issue on opencolin/nebius-builders. Tag @opencolin for routing.

items.title: DM the maintainer
items.text: Open a GitHub issue and @-mention @opencolin for anything time-sensitive or scoped to this build specifically.

items.title: Pull requests
items.text: Direct edits welcome — the build is opinionated but every choice is documented inline. CONTRIBUTING.md will land alongside the next port.

blocks.title: View the source
blocks.description: Every architectural decision in this build is documented inline in the source. The upstream content + types live in opencolin/nebius-builders; the Nebius-stack rebuild itself ships from this working tree.

title: Nebius for AI Builders
seo_description: From training and fine-tuning to production inference at scale — plus a community of builders shipping real work.

blocks.title: Nebius for AI Builders
blocks.description: From training and fine-tuning to production inference at scale. Plus a community of builders shipping real work — workshops, demos, hackathons, office hours. Get started with $100 of Token Factory or AI Cloud credits.

items.title: $100 in credits
items.text: Token Factory and AI Cloud intro credits when you sign up.

items.title: Builder community
items.text: Workshops, demos, hackathons, office hours — run by builders, for builders.

items.title: Recognition
items.text: Tier up from Builder → Contributor → Ambassador as you ship.

children.title: Run a workshop
children.text: Got a meetup, a hackathon, an office? We'll send swag, advocate support, and per-event credits to give your attendees.

children.title: Submit to the library
children.text: Tutorials, workshops, video walkthroughs, repos. We promote the best ones to the front page.

children.title: Apply for credits

list.text: Stack on top of free trial

blocks.title: Built on Nebius. Run by builders.
blocks.description: Every event, every workshop, every line of code in the showcase came from someone in the community. See what's coming up, and meet the team behind it.

title: The Nebius Builders Network
seo_description: Ambassadors, Contributors, and hackathon participants who shipped real projects on Nebius.

blocks.title: The Nebius Builders Network
blocks.description: Ambassadors, Contributors, and hackathon participants who shipped real projects at Nebius.Build SF and the JetBrains × Codex hackathon. Each one tied to a GitHub repo and one or more projects in the showcase.

items.title: L1 Builder
items.text: Anyone who's signed up. Get $100 in credits, +5 pts/month for shipping public work.

items.title: L2 Contributor
items.text: Hosted an event or had a library submission accepted. Per-event credits, deeper office hours.

items.title: L3 Ambassador
items.text: Recurring program lead — quarterly stipend, partner intros, joint launches.

children.title: Ship in public
children.text: Repos, blog posts, videos, conference talks, recurring meetups. We weight by quality and reach, not vanity metrics.

children.title: Teach others
children.text: Workshops, tutorials, code walkthroughs. Every accepted library submission earns points.

children.title: Grow the community
children.text: Refer other builders. Co-host with other companies. Run hackathon prizes. We pair people up across cities.

blocks.title: Ready to join?
blocks.description: 90-second sign-up, GitHub OAuth. Get $100 in credits and a +5 starter score.

title: Office Hours · Drop in. Get unstuck.
seo_description: Weekly office hours with the Nebius DevRel team. Bring your code, error, idea, or scoping question.

blocks.title: Drop in. Get unstuck.
blocks.description: Weekly office hours with the Nebius DevRel team. Bring your code, your error, your design question, or your half-baked idea. We'll help you debug, tune performance, pick the right model, scope an event, or just sanity-check what you're building. No agenda, no slides.

blocks.title: Three formats — pick what you need

items.title: Drop-in
items.text: No appointment needed. Show up to the published slot, ask your question, leave when you're unblocked. Group setting. Best for quick questions and demo-and-feedback loops.

items.title: 1-on-1
items.text: Book a 15–30 min slot in the advocate's calendar. Private, agenda is what you bring. Best for architecture reviews, event planning, partner intros, and longer code walk-throughs.

items.title: Group Q&A
items.text: Themed deep-dive on a specific Nebius topic — Token Factory throughput, Soperator on K8s, fine-tuning. RSVP via Luma. Slides-light, code-heavy.

blocks.title: Pick a person
blocks.description: Each team member runs their own recurring slots. Book directly with whoever covers your region or expertise area.

children.title: Half-finished agent
children.text: Bring it in any state. We'll help you scope, ship, or pivot.

children.title: Slow training job
children.text: Latency, throughput, GPU utilization — let's profile it together.

children.title: Confusing error
children.text: Logs, stack trace, repro steps. We'll dig in live.

children.title: Architecture sketch
children.text: Whiteboard photo or Figma — let's pressure-test it before you commit.

children.title: Event proposal
children.text: Topic, audience, venue. We'll help you scope and budget.

children.title: GPU bill that's too high
children.text: Walk us through your spend; we'll suggest what to tune.

blocks.title: Don't see a slot that works?
blocks.description: Reach out to Colin (Lead DevRel) — he handles unrouted requests and can pull in a Nebius engineer for technical questions outside DevRel's scope.

title: Localhosts · Run a Nebius event in your city
seo_description: Workshops, hackathons, demo nights — anywhere there's a builder community.

blocks.title: Run a Nebius event in your city
blocks.description: Workshops, hackathons, demo nights, office hours — anywhere there's a builder community. We send swag, code samples, advocate support, and per-event credits to give attendees.

items.title: Per-event credits
items.text: $50–500 of Token Factory or AI Cloud credits per attendee, depending on event size and format.

items.title: Content + slides
items.text: Workshop decks, code repos, recorded video walkthroughs you can adapt to your audience.

items.title: Co-marketing
items.text: We promote your event across the Nebius newsletter, dev social channels, and partner network.

children.title: Workshop
children.text: 60–120 min hands-on. We supply the curriculum (Token Factory inference, fine-tuning, agents, deploy patterns).

children.title: Hackathon
children.text: Half-day to weekend. Token Factory credits per team, prize sponsorship, optional advocate as a judge.

children.title: Demo night
children.text: 5-min lightning demos. Bring your community, show what you've built, swap notes.

blocks.title: How it works
blocks.description: Apply through the portal with date, venue, expected attendees. We respond within 48h with credit allocation, support level, and shipping logistics.
